title: adhatoda tristiflora
intro: Adhatoda tristiflora, also known as Vasa or Vasaka, is a tropical plant native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Adhatoda tristiflora include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Adhatoda tristiflora is commonly prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, as well as being used in Ayurvedic medicine in various forms, including capsules and extracts.
